description:
Filed 2026-05-25 from a code-walkthrough conversation surfacing that the `bare_squash` module's detached-worktree provisioning is redundant mechanism on top of a step (`merge_or_fail`) that already produces the right tree in the task worktree.

## What's there now

`bl review` flows through three sites:

1. `review::review_worktree` calls `merge_or_fail(&wt_path, &main_branch, ...)` — merges the integration branch *into* the task worktree's `work/<id>` branch. Conflicts are resolved here (where the agent has context). The worktree's `HEAD^{tree}` post-merge IS the tree the squash commit should ship.
2. `review::review_worktree` runs the quality gate (`run_pre_check`) against the same post-merge worktree.
3. `bare_squash::squash_into_main` produces the actual squash commit. Three branches:
   - **`squash_in_place`** (non-bare, integration branch == root checkout): `git merge --squash work/<id>` then `git commit`, both in `store.root`. Re-derives a tree the task worktree already has.
   - **`squash_in_detached_worktree`** (bare root OR target_branch != checkout, two flavors that hit the same code): provisions `<root>/.balls/local/squash-<pid>/` as a detached worktree at the integration branch's tip, runs `git merge --squash` there, commits, `update-ref refs/heads/<main>`, scrubs the worktree. The PID suffix is for concurrent-review safety.

Three code paths exist because `git merge --squash` is porcelain that requires a work tree, and the three cases have different "where do I get a work tree?" answers.

## What this ball changes

Collapse to one path using plumbing:

```rust
let merged_tree = git_rev_parse(&wt_path, "HEAD^{tree}")?;
let main_tip    = git_rev_parse(&wt_path, main_branch)?;
let new_sha     = git_commit_tree(&wt_path, &merged_tree, &[&main_tip], msg)?;
git_update_ref(&store.root, &format!("refs/heads/{main_branch}"), &new_sha)?;
```

`git commit-tree` is plumbing — no work tree, no index mutation; produces a commit object whose tree and parent are given by the args. The resulting commit is **bit-identical** to what `git merge --squash` + `git commit` produces: one parent (main's old tip), tree containing the merged work, no merge metadata to `work/<id>`.

`merge_or_fail` already wrote the right tree to the object database when it committed the merge into the task worktree; `commit-tree` re-uses that tree object directly.

## Working-tree sync for the in-place same-branch case

The current `squash_in_place` mutates `store.root`'s work tree as a side effect — after `bl review` the user's checkout at the repo root shows the new merged state. The plumbing approach moves the ref without touching the work tree.

Fix is one line after `update-ref`: if `store.root`'s checked-out branch is the one we just updated, `git reset --hard HEAD` to re-sync the work tree. For the bare case and the `target_branch != checkout` case, skip the reset — the root's working tree was never tracking this branch anyway, so leaving it alone is correct (and arguably an improvement over today's `squash_in_detached_worktree` which silently bypasses the user's checkout).

## What this collapses

- `bare_squash::squashes_in_place` predicate — gone.
- `bare_squash::squash_in_place` — gone.
- `bare_squash::squash_in_detached_worktree` — gone.
- `bare_squash::squash_worktree_path` — gone.
- `bare_squash::scrub_path` — gone.
- `bare_squash::worktree_add_detach` — gone.
- `bare_squash::is_bare_repo` — keeps living in some form (other callers reference it, e.g. `store_paths::require_git_repo`).
- `review_safety::rewind_main`'s `squashes_in_place` branch — collapses; rewind is always `update-ref refs/heads/<main> <pre_sha>` (with the optional `reset --hard` mirror for the same-branch case).

The `squash-<pid>/` orphan disappears from `Store::local_dir` consumers, shrinking bl-a11d's per-clone catch-all problem to two items (pending-sync, seen-claim-sync-policy).

## Conformance

- All existing `bl review` tests (`tests/review.rs`, `tests/review_squash.rs`, `tests/review_safety.rs`, `tests/bare_squash.rs`, `tests/review_pre_check.rs`, `tests/forge_deferred*.rs`) must pass unchanged. The squash commit is bit-identical; observed behavior should be identical save for the working-tree state at `store.root` post-review (covered by the one-line resync).
- One new test: verify that `bl review` on a non-bare clone with `main` checked out at the root leaves `store.root`'s working tree in sync with the new HEAD (catches the resync regression).
- The bare-clone path (`bl-56f4`'s motivating fixture) keeps working — the new path's bare case is the same `commit-tree + update-ref` pair the detached-worktree path winds up doing anyway.

## Concurrent safety

Today: two `bl review` calls in the same store provision distinct `squash-<pid>/` worktrees but contend on `git worktree add` against the same gitdir. Plumbing: each review reads its own task worktree's tree and writes a new commit object (object writes are content-addressed and inherently parallel). Only contention is the final `update-ref refs/heads/<main>`, which git serializes via the ref backend's atomic CAS. The plumbing path is *more* concurrent-safe than today's.

## Why not under bl-f021

The XDG epic and this refactor are logically independent. bl-f021 is "where do balls files live"; this is "how do we produce one specific commit." Filing standalone keeps the relocation epic from acquiring scope creep.

## Side benefit for bl-a11d

bl-a11d is deciding the home for three orphaned `local_dir` consumers (pending-sync, seen-claim-sync-policy, squash-<pid>). If this ball lands first, the third orphan vanishes — the per-clone catch-all problem shrinks to two items that fit cleanly in clone.json + a fifth SPEC dir (or one in clone.json, one in cache). bl-a11d should depend on this so its analysis works from the reduced surface.
title: Refactor bl review squash: replace detached-worktree with commit-tree plumbing
